heading: Certification regarding the use of certain Federal funds

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: a Prohibition
children.text: No Federal funds received under the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.) by an institution of higher education or other postsecondary educational institution may be used to pay any person for influencing or attempting to influence an officer or employee of any agency, a Member of Congress, an officer or employee of Congress, or an employee of a Member of Congress in connection with any Federal action described in subsection (b).20 U.S.C. 1001
The awarding of any Federal contract.
The making of any Federal grant.
The making of any Federal loan.
The entering into of any Federal cooperative agreement.
The extension, continuation, renewal, amendment, or modification of any Federal contract, grant, loan, or cooperative agreement.

children.heading: Lobbying and earmarks
children.label: c Lobbying and earmarks
children.text: No Federal student aid funding under the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.) may be used to hire a registered lobbyist or pay any person or entity for securing an earmark.20 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Certification
children.label: d Certification
children.text: Each institution of higher education or other postsecondary educational institution receiving Federal funding under the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.), as a condition for receiving such funding, shall annually certify to the Secretary of Education that the requirements of subsections (a) through (c) have been met.20 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Actions to implement and enforce
children.label: e Actions to implement and enforce
children.text: The Secretary of Education shall take such actions as are necessary to ensure that the provisions of this section are implemented and enforced.
